title: Finance Manager
description:
About the Company

A leading technology solutions provider delivering scalable, secure, and AI-enabled platforms to clients across the Asia-Pacific region. The company is at the forefront of cloud, SaaS, and IT innovation, driving digital transformation across industries.

Role Overview

The Finance Manager will play a pivotal role in driving financial consolidation, reporting, and strategic finance initiatives across the group. This role involves leading multi-entity and multi-region consolidation, ensuring compliance with IFRS/GAAP standards, and partnering closely with senior management to deliver actionable insights that inform business strategy. The position also contributes to budgeting, forecasting, and finance transformation initiatives, supporting the company’s growth in a fast-paced tech environment.

Key Responsibilities

  • Lead financial consolidation and reporting across multiple entities and regions in compliance with IFRS/GAAP.

  • Manage intercompany eliminations, foreign currency translations, and equity accounting to ensure accuracy and transparency.

  • Collaborate with regional finance teams to standardize reporting processes, ensure timely delivery, and maintain data integrity.

  • Provide financial insights and performance analysis to senior leadership, enabling strategic decision-making and business growth.

  • Drive continuous improvement in financial processes, including consolidation, forecasting, and finance transformation initiatives.

Qualifications & Experience

  • Bachelor’s degree in Accounting, Finance, or related field; CPA/CA qualification is a plus.

  • Minimum 6 years of relevant experience in financial reporting, consolidation, or audit, ideally within MNCs or tech/SaaS companies.

  • Strong technical knowledge of IFRS or local GAAP; hands-on experience in financial consolidation and reporting.

  • Proficient in Microsoft Office; experience with SAP, NetSuite, or other ERP systems preferred.

  • Excellent analytical, problem-solving, and communication skills, with proven stakeholder management capabilities.

Reg. No: R21103564

EA License: 23S1921

UNCOVERING HUMAN POTENTIAL AND CREATING POWERFUL PARTNERSHIPS THAT CHANGE THE FUTURE OF WORK. 

Tardis Group is a boutique human capital consultancy that prides itself on creating expert solutions for our clients. For 35 years, we have been trusted advisors shaping the world of work, connecting exceptional talent with world class organisations. With deep industry expertise, we mobilise talent globally working across seven pivotal locations Sydney, New York, London, Tokyo, Hong Kong, Perth & Singapore.
